title: ClearnessQuery
description: P-VS-07 (Clearness committee) phase-1 query output. Open queries — never leading questions. Per Loring 1992 PHP #305 + Brown 2017 PHP #446 + Fox 1657 Journal. The phase-1 output (this schema's array) becomes context for phase-2 verdict.

text.description: The query text itself. ≥15 chars. Open form: 'What evidence supports the claim in §3 that X implies Y?' NOT leading: 'Don't you think §3 is wrong?'

is_leading.description: MUST be false. A leading query (pre-loads its answer) is a phase-1 violation per P-VS-07. The auto-J classifier check (per A6 §9 falsifier #4) verifies ≥95% rate of is_leading=false across all phase-1 queries.

source_query_template.description: If derived from BYM Advices & Queries, FGC primary docs, or a few-shot example, cite the source for traceability. e.g., 'BYM Advices & Queries §1.02'.

anchor_section.description: Optional anchor in the candidate work this query targets. e.g., '§3' or 'the assertion at line 47'.
